generated_comment: Arre, isme issue kya hai? Jo jahan better hai wahi sambhale na—main finances, bank, bahar ke kaam dekh leta hoon, aur ghar ki planning, khana-vana, bachchon ki routine wife naturally better handle karti hai. Weekend pe chai bana du ya kabhi market se samaan le aaun toh help ho jaati hai, but daily kitchen mein ghuske kya practicality hai?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames a gendered division of labor as natural and efficient rather than unequal. It encodes the 'he handles outside/finances, she handles home' pattern and the 'he helps her' framing instead of shared responsibility.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
